prevalence.source: FBG (Federação Brasileira de Gastroenterologia) e DataSUS
prevalence.trend: increasing due to obesity epidemic and westernized dietary habits

riskFactors: dietary habits (high-fat meals, common in Brazilian churrasco culture)
vulnerablePopulations: individuals with low socioeconomic status in urban areas
epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Lyon Consensus criteria adapted for Brazil, incorporating Rome IV for functional dyspepsia overlap

publicHealthSystem.controlRate: 65% with PPI therapy adherence via SUS

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Clinical diagnosis based on symptoms, endoscopy recommended when alarm symptoms (e.g., weight loss, dysphagia) present
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: PPIs (omeprazole) first-line via SUS, lifestyle modifications (weight loss, dietary changes), H2 blockers as alternatives
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Limited endoscopy access in rural and northern regions, obesity epidemic (prevalence >20% in adults) increasing cases, high consumption of trigger foods like fatty meats
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Free PPI access via SUS for low-income patients, FBG clinical guidelines promoting evidence-based management
